questions.question: What defines a controlled input in Vue?
options: An input whose value is stored in a Pinia store
An input whose value is driven by reactive state via `v-model`
An input that validates its own value without external logic
An input rendered by a form library like VeeValidate

questions.question: For which type of input is the uncontrolled pattern the only practical option?
options: Text inputs with real-time character count
Select inputs with dynamic options loaded from an API
File inputs, because `<input type="file">` is read-only and `v-model` does not apply
Password inputs, because Vue prevents binding sensitive values reactively

questions.question: What does `v-model.lazy` do compared to the default `v-model`?
options: It defers the initial render until the input is focused
It syncs the value on the `change` event (blur/enter) instead of on every `input` event
It prevents the input from triggering a re-render until submit
It trims whitespace from the value before updating the reactive ref
